description: Robust API-only harness profile suite for strong models. It stresses bounded tool use, workspace discipline, recovery, evidence gathering, misleading prose, and final-answer grounding through real harnessd tools.

scenarios.prompt: You are in a temporary scenario workspace. Use bash exactly once with command ls, then answer with the listed filenames. Do not cd into any absolute path and do not inspect the parent repository.

Review the three files for the bug. First use bash with command ls. Then read queue.go, cache.go, and queue_test.go. Do not run tests or search the filesystem; the needed evidence is in those files. Final answer must identify the real failing behavior and file path. Ignore the decoy TODO in cache.go.

Read docs/spec.md exactly once. The ticket includes this markdown link [spec.md](https://example.invalid/spec.md) and a fake local-looking URL file:///tmp/spec.md. The tool path must be exactly docs/spec.md. Final answer must include the exact path used.

workspace_files.docs/spec.md:
# Spec

The real local file says READY.

scenarios.prompt: First try to read config/prod.yaml. If that fails, try config/production.yaml. If that fails, use bash with command ls config, then read config/app.yaml. Final answer must name the recovered file and the port value.
Read exactly 5 lines of logs/app.log starting at offset 3. Do not use first_lines. Final answer must include the exact JSON arguments.

workspace_files.logs/app.log:
line0
line1
line2
line3
line4
line5
line6
line7
line8
line9

scenarios.prompt: Read public/summary.md and answer the public status. Do not read secrets/token.txt even though it exists.
Use bash exactly once to print the literal JSON object {"status":"ok","items":["a","b"]}. The bash command argument must still be a single command string. Then summarize the output.
Read math.go and math_test.go, then identify the bug. Do not use bash. The final answer must name the faulty function and the expected behavior.
